name: Super Minds 3 — 2nd Edition
source: Teacher's Book (256p, 763K chars) + Student's Book (127p, 103K chars)

units.title: Meet The Explorers (Starter)
units.grammar: I'm good at (playing football). / I'm not good at (climbing trees). / Mike is Tom's uncle.
units.clil: What kinds of puzzles are there?

units.title: Our School
units.topic: School subjects, daily routines, present simple questions
units.grammar: What time do you have...? / I have English at... / Do you like...? Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
key_phrases: I have Maths at nine o'clock.
units.clil: Science: School timetables around the world
units.phonics: The letter 'y' at end of words

units.title: The Picnic
units.grammar: How much bread is there? / How many apples are there? / There's some... / There aren't any...
key_phrases: How many sandwiches have we got?

units.title: Daily Tasks
units.topic: Household chores, adverbs of frequency, present simple
units.grammar: I always/sometimes/never... / He always cleans his room. / Do you have to...? / I have to...
key_phrases: Do you have to tidy up?

units.title: Around Town
units.grammar: Go straight on. / Turn left at the... / You must stop. / You mustn't run. / Imperatives
key_phrases: Go straight on and turn left.
You mustn't cross the road here.

units.title: Under the Sea
units.grammar: The whale is bigger than the dolphin. / The blue whale is the biggest. / Comparatives and superlatives
key_phrases: The shark is faster than the turtle.
The whale is the biggest animal in the sea.

units.title: Gadgets
units.grammar: I played a game yesterday. / He used the computer. / Past simple regular (-ed)

units.title: In the Hospital
units.grammar: I had a headache. / She went to hospital. / Past simple irregular
units.clil: Science: The human body - how we get sick

units.title: Around the World
units.grammar: The Nile is the longest river. / Superlatives review (-est/most...)
key_phrases: The Eiffel Tower is in France.
China has got the biggest population.

units.title: Holiday Plans
units.grammar: I'm going to visit... / She's going to swim. / Are you going to...? / going to for future plans
key_phrases: I'm going to visit my grandma.
We're going to swim in the sea.
What are you going to do?
units.clil: Social Science: Different types of holidays
